title: GTA 6 Characters | Jason Duval and Lucia Caminos, Every Confirmed Detail
subtitle: GTA 6 introduces dual protagonists for the first time in franchise history. Jason Duval and Lucia Caminos are confirmed by name, their dynamic is Bonnie and Clyde, and Lucia is the first female lead in a mainline Grand Theft Auto game.
subject: GTA 6
description: Full breakdown of GTA 6's confirmed characters: Jason Duval and Lucia Caminos, their confirmed story roles, the dual protagonist structure, how they compare to GTA 5's three protagonists, and every detail Rockstar has shown in Trailer 1 and Trailer 2.

sources.title: IGN — GTA 6 Character Details and Trailer Analysis

timeline.description: Trailer 1 drops. Lucia Caminos appears in the opening shot in a prison work program. A voiceover quotes her: "I know you ain't running on me. We're like Bonnie and Clyde." Jason is seen with her in later shots. Neither name is stated on screen.

Trailer 2 released. Jason Duval and Lucia Caminos are named on screen for the first time. Extended sequences show both protagonists operating across Vice City. Their criminal partnership and personal dynamic are made explicit.
GTA 6 launches on PS5 and Xbox Series X|S. Full character backstories, voice actors, and story details will be revealed at launch.

GTA 6 Trailer 2 Full Breakdown | Every Detail Confirmed by Rockstar

content_html:
GTA 6 introduces dual protagonists for the first time in franchise history with Jason Duval and Lucia Caminos, confirmed by Rockstar Games through two official trailers. Lucia is the first female protagonist in a mainline Grand Theft Auto game. Both characters operate as criminal partners in the State of Leonida, a fictionalized South Florida setting. Everything confirmed about Jason and Lucia is sourced directly from Trailer 1 (December 2023), Trailer 2 (May 2025), and official Rockstar statements. Voice actors and detailed backstories have not been released as of May 2026.

GTA 6 PROTAGONISTS AT A GLANCE

  • Jason Duval — Male protagonist. Criminal partner to Lucia. Named on screen in Trailer 2.
  • Lucia Caminos — Female protagonist. First female lead in a mainline GTA game. Named on screen in Trailer 2. Shown in prison work program at start of Trailer 1.
  • Relationship: Criminal partnership, implicitly romantic. "Bonnie and Clyde" reference from Trailer 1 voiceover.
  • Setting: State of Leonida (South Florida). Both operate across Vice City and rural areas.
  • Voice actors: Not announced by Rockstar as of May 2026.
  • Switchable protagonists: Not confirmed. GTA 5 allowed free switching between 3 characters.

1. Lucia Caminos | The First Female Protagonist in Mainline GTA

Lucia Caminos is the character shown in the opening shot of GTA 6 Trailer 1. She is seen in an outdoor work detail in what appears to be the Everglades, wearing a work-release jumpsuit on a rural road flanked by correctional officers and other inmates. The shot establishes her in a position of constrained circumstances that she is clearly working to escape, setting the tone for her character arc before a single line of dialogue lands.

Lucia's first line in Trailer 1 is delivered as a voiceover: "I know you ain't running on me. We're like Bonnie and Clyde, but like, if they had a real shot at it." This line confirms her relationship with Jason and frames the game's central premise as a criminal-couple survival story with aspirational stakes. The phrasing, "a real shot at it," suggests a narrative preoccupied with escape, legitimacy, or a better life beyond crime.

CONFIRMED

  • Full name: Lucia Caminos
  • Female protagonist (franchise first)
  • Shown in prison/work release
  • Criminal partner to Jason
  • Operates in State of Leonida

NOT CONFIRMED

  • Voice actor
  • Backstory / origin details
  • Specific crime specialization
  • Whether she is the default protagonist
  • Age, nationality

The name Lucia Caminos is the first confirmed female protagonist name in the mainline Grand Theft Auto series. Prior GTA games have had female playable characters in side-missions and multiplayer modes, but the 3D and HD universe mainline titles (GTA III, Vice City, San Andreas, IV, V) have exclusively featured male protagonists. Lucia's inclusion is the most significant structural character change in the franchise in over 25 years.

2. Jason Duval | What Trailers Reveal

Jason Duval is the male protagonist, Lucia's criminal partner, and the second character confirmed by name in Trailer 2. Compared to Lucia, Jason has less prominent screen time across the two trailers, with Trailer 1 focusing more heavily on Lucia's perspective and Trailer 2 sharing the focus more evenly.

Jason is shown across multiple sequence types in Trailer 2: driving through Vice City at night, in what appears to be a suburban property during a daylight sequence, and in higher-intensity action shots that suggest he handles the more physically confrontational aspects of their partnership. His visual design projects a deliberate casualness, athletic build, civilian clothing that codes him as local rather than criminal-industrial.

JASON DUVAL CONFIRMED DETAILS

  • Full name: Jason Duval (confirmed in Trailer 2)
  • Role: Male protagonist, Lucia's criminal partner
  • Visual: Athletic build, casual civilian wardrobe, South Florida aesthetic
  • Sequences shown: Driving in Vice City, suburban settings, high-action scenes
  • Voice actor: Not announced
  • Background: Not disclosed by Rockstar

The surname Duval is a French-origin surname common in Louisiana and South Florida, consistent with Leonida's Francophone geographic echoes (Duval County encompasses Jacksonville in real Florida, while the Keys and South Beach have significant French-Caribbean cultural presence). Whether this surname is meaningful to Jason's backstory, or incidental, is unknown.

3. The Dual Protagonist Structure | How It Works and Why It Matters

GTA 6 is the second mainline GTA game to feature multiple protagonists after GTA 5 (2013), which introduced three playable characters: Michael De Santa, Trevor Philips, and Franklin Clinton. The reduction from three to two protagonists in GTA 6 reflects a deliberate design philosophy shift: depth over breadth.

Game Protagonists Switch Mechanic Narrative Approach
GTA 5 (2013) 3 (Michael, Trevor, Franklin) Free radial switch Parallel lives, occasionally converging
Red Dead Redemption 2 (2018) 1 (Arthur Morgan) N/A Single arc, deep characterization
GTA 6 (2026) 2 (Jason, Lucia) Not confirmed Paired criminal arc, "Bonnie and Clyde" framing

The GTA 5 three-protagonist system was structurally praised but created a narrative dilution problem: no single character's story had enough screen time to build the emotional depth that a single-protagonist game achieves. Michael's suburban disconnection, Trevor's psychopathy, and Franklin's ambition were all compelling setups that the three-way structure could not fully resolve. GTA 6's reduction to two suggests Rockstar recognized this limitation.

The closest analogue in Rockstar's portfolio to a two-protagonist structure with romantic criminal partnership framing is not from the GTA series at all — it is the Arthur Morgan and Dutch van der Linde dynamic in Red Dead Redemption 2, where two characters in a partnership of mutual dependency drive the central dramatic tension. GTA 6 appears to literalize that structure by making both figures playable.

WHY THIS MATTERS

A male-female dual protagonist structure in GTA 6 has implications beyond character count. It changes mission design, since some scenarios may require both characters or allow players to choose who approaches a target. It changes the emotional architecture of the story, since a romantic criminal partnership creates stakes that a crew-of-strangers structure does not. And it changes GTA's cultural footprint: Lucia Caminos is the most significant new GTA character announcement since CJ in San Andreas, and her reception among a broader audience than the franchise's historical core demographic is already evident from the Trailer 1 response.

4. Supporting Characters | What Trailers Have Shown

Beyond Jason and Lucia, GTA 6's trailers show an extensive cast of unnamed supporting characters and NPCs. Rockstar has not officially named any supporting character beyond the two protagonists. The following figures appear in the trailers with enough prominence to be significant:

  • Law enforcement figures: Multiple police, sheriff, and paramilitary-style officers appear across both trailers. A law enforcement chase sequence in Trailer 2 involves armored vehicles, suggesting a high-threat level police response system.
  • Criminal network figures: Several individuals in both trailers appear to be criminal associates rather than antagonists, suggesting Jason and Lucia operate within a larger criminal ecosystem rather than as isolated operators.
  • Influencer-coded NPCs: Trailer 1 shows social media-posting NPCs in party and beach settings. These figures appear to be reflective of Vice City's social scene rather than named characters, but their prominence suggests the influencer culture of South Florida is woven into the game's satirical fabric.
  • Possible antagonists: At least one sequence in Trailer 2 shows Jason and Lucia apparently being pursued or threatened by a figure who is not law enforcement. A primary antagonist has not been named or shown by Rockstar.

5. What Has Not Been Confirmed

?

Voice actors for Jason and Lucia

Rockstar has not announced casting. The voices heard in trailers are presumably the final performances, but actors have not been publicly credited.

?

Whether players can switch freely between Jason and Lucia

GTA 5 allowed radial switching between its three protagonists at any time outside of missions. Whether GTA 6 enables free switching, mission-gated switching, or a linear dual narrative is unconfirmed.

?

Whether Jason and Lucia have unique skill sets

GTA 5's three protagonists each had different stat strengths (Michael: shooting, Trevor: toughness, Franklin: driving). GTA 6 characters likely have differentiators, but none have been described.

?

Primary antagonist names

No GTA 6 antagonist has been named by Rockstar. The game's trailers show law enforcement and criminal threats but have not introduced a named villain.

?

Jason and Lucia's fate at the end of the game

The Bonnie and Clyde reference historically foreshadows tragedy. Whether GTA 6's story ends with the pair escaping, separating, or dying is pure speculation — and exactly the kind of ambiguity Rockstar cultivates before launch.

Frequently Asked Questions

Who are the GTA 6 characters?

GTA 6 has two confirmed protagonists: Jason Duval and Lucia Caminos. Lucia is the first female protagonist in a mainline GTA game. Both are criminal partners operating in the State of Leonida, Rockstar's fictionalized South Florida.

Who is Lucia in GTA 6?

Lucia Caminos is a female protagonist in GTA 6 and the first female lead in the mainline Grand Theft Auto series. She is shown in a prison work program at the start of Trailer 1 and named on screen in Trailer 2. Her criminal partnership with Jason forms the game's central relationship.

Who is Jason in GTA 6?

Jason Duval is the male protagonist of GTA 6 and Lucia's criminal partner. He is named on screen in Trailer 2. His backstory and voice actor have not been officially announced by Rockstar as of May 2026.

Can you play as both Jason and Lucia?

Rockstar has confirmed both characters are protagonists but has not explained the switching mechanic. GTA 5 allowed free switching between its three protagonists. Whether GTA 6 uses the same system or a different structure is not confirmed.

What is the Bonnie and Clyde reference in GTA 6?

In GTA 6 Trailer 1, Lucia's voiceover says: "We're like Bonnie and Clyde, but like, if they had a real shot at it." The reference frames Jason and Lucia as a criminal couple with aspirational stakes, suggesting the story is about whether they can succeed where the real Bonnie and Clyde did not.

This article will be updated when Rockstar Games releases new character details. Last updated May 25, 2026. Reported by Jack Sterling, Gaming Correspondent. For the full GTA 6 hub, see the GTA 6 complete guide.
